delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
Students will develop practical communicative presentation competence & learn to express more scientific ideas in English.
Students are expected to explain/communicate & give SHORT PRESENTATIONS expressing themselves using technical/biology related ENGLISH.
Students are expected to learn to THINK & GIVE simple scientific answers using ENGLISH.
Lesson content used in class will also be posted on moodle.
Classes are basically delivered live in class, but sometimes may be on-demand.
aims: Have fun improving your English communication/presentation ability using scientific topics related to biology.
goals:
Students will be able to convey complex technical data in English using basic information structures.
Students will develop practical communicative presentation competence & learn to express more scientific ideas in English.

Students will be able to create English language presentations.
Students will be able to explain/communicate & give SHORT PRESENTATIONS expressing themselves using technical/environmental/biological-related ENGLISH.

Students will be able to have simple, informal conversations which are important to building international relationships.
Students will understand and be able to communicate about biology and the environment in English.

plan.text:
Introduction/ Class Orientation
Description & Information Mapping
English Written/Spoken Self-introduction using Information Mapping

Description & Information Mapping Presentation [5%]
Oral & Written Self-introduction/Famous Scientific Person Presentation
Using Description & Information Mapping
Look at audience
Point at info-map
Do NOT read!
SDGs gender equality

Sequence
In groups (individually) make an info-map & sentences on biology/bio-tech/chemistry sequence of events

Biology/Bio-tech/Chemistry Sequence Presentation [10%]
Good/correct pronunciation
Look at audience
Point at info-map

Cause & Effect
In groups (individually) make an info-map & sentences on biology/bio-tech/chemistry cause & effect systems/events
SDGs life below water/life on land

Cause & Effect Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice
Look at audience
Point at info-map

Inference
In groups (individually) make drawing (an info-map) & sentences on biology/bio-tech/chemistry inference of events/mystery
SDGs reduce inequality

Inference MIDTERM EXAM Presentation [20%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing
Relax

Comparison
Do group (individual) comparison research on biology/bio-tech/chemistry systems/events

Comparison Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing
Relax

Graphs & Data Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice/ good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at graph
Relax

Pros & Cons
--Topic: PROS & CONS of scientific related thing/event/question
[Final Group (Individual) EXAM Presentation]
SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities

Pros & Cons [Pure vs. Applied Bio-tech/ Chemistry]
--Topic: PROS & CONS of scientific related thing/event/question

Review/Prepare/Do FINAL Group (individual) EXAM PRESENTATION: Written & Oral Report [25%]
--Topic: PROS & CONS of scientific related thing/event/question
(SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities etc.)

Final EXAM PRESENTATION: Written & Oral Report [25%]
--Topic: PROS & CONS of scientific related thing/event/question
(SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities etc.)

textbooks:
Original materials will be distributed in each class &
posted on the KU moodle website [https://moodle.kochi-u.ac.jp/2026/]
prereq: This course is mainly for 2nd-year students in the science faculty who have mastered basic 1st-year English conversation & have a prior knowledge of biology/chemistry/bio-tech/natural sciences.
prep: Students are expected to review/prepare for each class using a DICTIONARY/PC/moodle. Students are expected to keep a FOLDER/NOTEBOOK with notes & class handouts. Students will be evaluated in every class on homework, in-class activities, quizzes or presentations; therefore ATTENDANCE/ PARTICIPATION is very important.
teachers: ◎ フェビアン ポーラ ダイアン

extra.label: 【テーマ（英語）】(IN ENGLISH)
extra.text: Talking about & giving presentations in English using biology-related topics.

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
専門分野に関する知識
論理的思考力
情報に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label:
履修に係わる注意事項
【NOTES ON CLASS ENROLLMENT】
extra.text:
It is very important for students to ATTEND class regularly (**MAX 3 absences), especially the 1st introductory lesson.
Students are expected to ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE individually & in groups from the very 1st lesson.
Students are expected to ALWAYS be prepared to speak/communicate to each other in ENGLISH.
Doing both CLASSWORK & HOMEWORK are a vital part of succeeding in this course.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
55% In-class PRESENTATIONS/ Work/ Quizzes
Participation/ Homework/ Preparation (Attendance is important: maximum 3 absences)
45%: 20% Midterm EXAM Presentation + 25% Final EXAM PRESENTATION

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連
Have worked as a teacher; teaching in kindergarten, elementary school NPO summer programs, junior & senior high school, vocational schools, cram schools, language schools & university. Before teaching, I worked for a small company in public relations/marketing & advertising. Over the years have volunteered or worked in/with NPO groups.

extra.label: 授業形態
extra.text:
講義形式が中心
口頭での質疑やディスカッションを含む
授業中や授業時間外のグループワークを含む
その他
Some classes will meet online or on demand on moodle
